title: Every date field gets a future-date-blocking constraint
description: Every survey row with type=date gets constraint=`. <= today()` and constraint_message='Future dates are not allowed.' This applies to all forms, all events, all domains. A small set of date fields are intentionally future-dated (projected discharge date, scheduled-but-not-yet-completed visit date); for these, do NOT apply the constraint and tag the row with flag_reason='Future date allowed — scheduled/projected' so the reviewer sees the deviation.
rationale: Clinical-event dates by definition record something that has happened. A future date in such a field is either a typo or a data-entry slip. Catching it at entry rather than in monitoring saves the deviation report and the corrective-action cycle. The exception for scheduled/planned dates is narrow and should be flagged for review when used.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} (type=date) pinned with `. <= today()` constraint. Verify this is NOT a scheduled/planned-date field (projected discharge, planned visit) where future values are legitimate; if so, override at study scope and tag the row with flag_reason='Future date allowed — scheduled/projected'.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §2 during Phase B.1d. Overlaps with OC-7 sub-rule 7B (clinical_patterns.start_date_not_in_future) — §2 is the general rule for ALL date fields; 7B is the *STDAT-specific application with a slightly stricter constraint-message convention. Both coexist: §2 fires universally on type=date; 7B fires on *STDAT-named fields and *STDAT-pattern event dates. When both fire, the ensure directives are idempotent (same value), and 7B's flag adds the start-date-specific review note. Hybrid because the scheduled/planned-date exception requires per-field judgment — handled via the flag's review prompt rather than an applies_when carve-out (we don't have a reliable structural signal for 'this date is planned/projected').
